zh.lifeSummary: 當Ludwig VII. Graf von Oettingen於大約1270年出生在德國貝也藍德可萊斯 堂瑙-利斯哈布格時，他的父親Ludwig von OETTINGEN V32歲，母親Maria von Hohenzollern Nürnberg25歲。他於1288年9月3日之前與Anna Countess Of Dornberg結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1346年9月29日在奧地利維恩享年77歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Ludwig VII. Graf von Oettingen于大约1270年出生在德国貝也藍德可萊斯 堂瑙-利斯哈布格时，他的父亲Ludwig von OETTINGEN V32岁，母亲Maria von Hohenzollern Nürnberg25岁。他于1288年9月3日之前与Anna Countess Of Dornberg结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1346年9月29日在奥地利維恩享年77岁。

nameMeanings.name: Graf
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Otto, Erwin, Ernst, Fritz, Gerhard, Hermann, Horst, Manfred, Udo, Alois.

German (also Gräf): status name from Middle High German grāve, grābe, which was used as a title denoting various more or less aristocratic dignitaries and officials. In later times it became established as a title of nobility equivalent to the Romance count. The vocabulary word also denoted a variety of different minor local functionaries in different parts of Germany. In the Grand Duchy of Hesse, for example, it was used for the holder of the comparatively humble office of village headman (compare Mayer , Schulz , and Vogt ). The surname could have originated from any of these senses or be a metonymic occupational or status name for a servant or retainer of a count, or a nickname for someone who gave himself airs and graces. This surname is also found in many other European countries, for example in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Hungary, Poland, Czechia, Slovakia, Croatia, and Slovenia, often as a translation into German of the corresponding Hungarian and Slavic surnames Gróf and Grof . Compare Graef .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name selected, like   Herzog   and other words denoting titles, because of their aristocratic connotations.

variants.name: Grebe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Erwin, Fritz, Hans, Willi.
German (Hesse): variant of   Graf  .

variants.name: De Graaf
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Gerrit, Adriaan, Bastiaan, Cornelis, Dirk, Geert.

Dutch: from graaf ‘count’ (see Graf ), with the addition of the definite article de ‘the’, hence a nickname for the servant of a count or for someone supposedly resembling one, and also a topographic or habitational name referring to a house or inn named with this word.

variants.name: Graefe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Armin, Arno, Goetz, Horst, Johannes.
German (North Rhine-Westphalia; also Gräfe): variant of Graf 1.

variants.name: Grefe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erhardt, Gerhard, Gerhart, Kurt.
North German: variant of   Graf  .

variants.name: Greve
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerhard, Otto, Reinhold, Bernhardt, Claus, Erna, Erwin, Ewald, Fritz, Kurt.
North German and Scandinavian: status name from Middle Low German and Danish greve ‘count’, equivalent to German Graf .
Dutch: vowel variant of Grave ‘count’, or a topographic name from Middle Dutch greve ‘ditch, moat, channel’.

variants.name: Croff
meanings: Altered form of German   Graf   or   Graff  . Or of Dutch   Kroft  .

variants.name: Mayer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Hans, Franz, Helmut, Wilhelm, Erwin, Gerhard, Heinz, Ilse, Lorenz, Wolfgang.

English (Staffordshire): post-medieval form of Mear, which is either a topographic name, from Middle English mere ‘pond, pool’ (Old English mere) or Middle English mere ‘boundary’ (Old English (ge)mǣre), or a habitational name from one or more of the many places with names derived from these terms, such as Mere (Cheshire, Lincolnshire, Wiltshire), Meir in Caverswall (Staffordshire), and Meare (Somerset).
English: occupational name for a physician, from Old French mire, Middle English mire, meir, meyre, originally synonymous with Myer .

variants.name: Grafe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Fritz, Ralf.
German (also Gräfe): variant of Graf 1.

variants.name: Graef
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Egon, Lothar, Otto, Armin, Claus, Erwin, Franz, Friedrich, Hans, Helmut, Hermann, Jochen.
German (also Gräf): see Graf .
Dutch: variant of Graaf, see De Graaf .

variants.name: Graff
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) (also Gräff): variant of Graf . Compare Graeff .

English (London): metonymic occupational name for a clerk or scribe, from Old French grafe ‘stylus, pencil’, a derivative of Old French grafer ‘to write’ (from Late Latin grafare, from Greek graphein). It is uncertain whether the English name survived the medieval period or whether the early 17th century bearers in Britain were in fact of German origin.

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Oettingen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hanni, Christoph, Siegbert.
German (also Öttinger): habitational name for someone from a place in Bavaria called Oettingen or a place in Lorraine (France) called Öttingen (French Ottange). Compare Ottinger and Ettinger .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Ettinger  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Ottinger
meanings: German: variant of   Otting  .
German (also Öttinger): habitational name for someone from any of the places called Otting or Öttingen, in Swabia, Bavaria, and Lorraine (France). Compare Oettinger .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Ettinger  , a cognate of 2 above.

nameMeanings.name: Ludwig
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from hlud ‘fame’ + wīg ‘war’; an ancient royal and imperial name. See Louis .

variants.name: Louis
meanings: name, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hlōd ‘fame’ + wīg ‘war’. It was very common in French royal and noble families. Louis I ( 778–840 ) was the son of Charlemagne, who ruled as both King of France and Holy Roman Emperor. Altogether, the name was borne by sixteen kings of France up to the French Revolution, in which Louis XVI perished. Louis XIV, ‘the Sun King’ ( 1638–1715 ), reigned for seventy-two years ( 1643–1715 ), presiding in the middle part of his reign over a period of unparalleled French power and prosperity. In modern times Louis is also found in the English-speaking world (usually pronounced ‘loo-ee’). In Britain the Anglicized form Lewis is rather more common, whereas in America the reverse is true.

nameMeanings.name: VII.
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Victor .

variants.name: Victor
meanings: From a Late Latin personal name meaning ‘conqueror’. This was popular among early Christians as a reference to Christ's victory over death and sin, and was borne by several saints. An influence on the choice of the name in more recent times was the American actor  Victor Mature  (  1915–99 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Ludwig VII. Graf von Oettingen was born about 1270, in Harburg, Donau-Ries, Bavaria, Germany, his father, Ludwig von OETTINGEN V, was 32 and his mother, Maria von Hohenzollern Nürnberg, was 25. He married Anna Countess Of Dornberg before 3 September 1288. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 29 September 1346, in Vienna, Austria, at the age of 77.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ludwig VII. Graf von Oettingen est né environ 1270, à Harburg, Landkreis Donau-Ries, Bayern, Allemagne, son père, Ludwig von OETTINGEN V, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Maria von Hohenzollern Nürnberg, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Anna Countess Of Dornberg avant 3 septembre 1288. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 29 septembre 1346, à Vienne, Autriche, à l’âge de 77 ans.

de.Schwaben, Kelheim, Landkreis Kelheim, Ba: Schwaben, Kelheim, Landkreis Kelheim, Bayern, Deutschland
de.lifeSummary: Als Ludwig VII. Graf von Oettingen um 1270 in Harburg, Landkreis Donau-Ries, Bayern, Deutschland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Ludwig von OETTINGEN V 32 und seine Mutter Maria von Hohenzollern Nürnberg 25. Er hat vor 3. September 1288 Anna Countess Of Dornberg geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 29. September 1346 im Alter von 77 Jahren in Wien, Österreich.

Graf von Oettingen è nato il circa 1270 nel Harburg, Landkreis Donau-Ries, Bayern, Germania, il suo padre, Ludwig von OETTINGEN V, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Maria von Hohenzollern Nürnberg, aveva 25 anni. Lui sposato Anna Countess Of Dornberg prima del 3 settembre 1288. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 29 settembre 1346, nel Wien, Austria, all'età di 77 anni.

ko.Schwaben, Kelheim, Landkreis Kelheim, Ba: 독일 베이른 랜드크레이스 켈하임 켈하임 슈와벤
ko.lifeSummary: Ludwig VII. Graf von Oettingen(이)가 대략 1270년 독일 베이른 랜드크레이스 도나-리스 하르버그에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Ludwig von OETTINGEN V(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Maria von Hohenzollern Nürnberg(은)는 25세였다. 그는 1288년 9월 3일 이전 Anna Countess Of Dornberg(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1346년 9월 29일 오스트리아 빈에서 77세의 나이로 사망했다.

Graf von Oettingen nasceu aproximadamente 1270, em Harburg, Landkreis Donau-Ries, Bayern, Alemanha, seu pai, Ludwig von OETTINGEN V, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Maria von Hohenzollern Nürnberg, tinha 25 anos. Ele casou-se com Anna Countess Of Dornberg antes 3 de setembro de 1288. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 29 de setembro de 1346, em Wien, Áustria, com 77 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ludwig VII. Graf von Oettingen родился около 1270 г. в местности харбург, ландкрис донау-риесь, беиирн, Германия, его отцу по имени Ludwig von OETTINGEN V было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Maria von Hohenzollern Nürnberg, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Anna Countess Of Dornberg перед 3 сентябрь 1288 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 29 сентября 1346 г. в местности Вена, Австрия в возрасте 77 лет.

Se casó con Anna Countess Of Dornberg antes de 3 de septiembre de 1288. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 29 de septiembre de 1346, en Wien, Austria, a la edad de 77 años.
